title: Property path query not executed properly
description:
Hi,
I'm currently playing aournd with SPARQL 1.1 Property Paths, and just found a problem with this toy query on DBpedia (http://dbpedia.org/sparql) which is based on Virtuoso version 07.20.3215 on Linux (i686-generic-linux-glibc212-64), Single Server Edition:
select * where {?s <foo>|!<foo> ?o.} limit 10

It's not meaningful but should indeed just return some subjects and objects that are related by some predicate.
The exception I get:
Virtuoso 37000 Error SP031: SPARQL compiler: The list of return values contains '*' but the pattern does not contain variables

SPARQL query:
define sql:big-data-const 0
#output-format:text/html
define sql:signal-void-variables 1 define input:default-graph-uri <http://dbpedia.org> select * where {dbr:Berlin <foo>|!<foo> ?o.} limit 10

click
It also fails when I bind the subject to a specific resource and use ?o as projection variable, e.g.
select ?o where {dbr:Berlin <foo>|!<foo> ?o.} limit 10

click
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_property_paths (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, subject VARCHAR, predicate VARCHAR, object VARCHAR);
INSERT INTO test_property_paths (id, subject, predicate, object) VALUES (1, 'dbr:Berlin', '<foo>', 'dbr:Paris');
INSERT INTO test_property_paths (id, subject, predicate, object) VALUES (2, 'dbr:Berlin', '<foo>', 'dbr:London');
SELECT subject, object FROM test_property_paths WHERE predicate = '<foo>' OR predicate = '!<foo>' LIMIT 10;
SELECT DISTINCT object FROM test_property_paths WHERE subject = 'dbr:Berlin' AND (predicate = '<foo>' OR predicate = '!<foo>') LIMIT 10;
key_bug_features: SPARQL Property Paths with wildcard return values
